primary_accounting_issue: Hedge accounting for foreign exchange risk on intragroup dividend receivables in consolidated financial statements

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs establish the eligibility of recognized assets as hedged items, the restriction to external transactions in consolidated statements, and the exception for intragroup monetary items.

These paragraphs prescribe the recognition of exchange differences on monetary items in profit or loss and the option for recognition in other comprehensive income for items forming part of a net investment.
This Interpretation clarifies that hedge accounting may be applied only to foreign exchange differences arising between the functional currencies of the parent and the foreign operation.

supporting_authority.reason: These paragraphs set out the hedge accounting criteria and the reclassification of gains or losses upon disposal.
These paragraphs illustrate the interaction between hedge accounting and exchange difference recognition, and the treatment of intragroup monetary items in consolidation.
These paragraphs address the assessment of hedge effectiveness and the impact of the consolidation method.

peripheral_authority.reason: These paragraphs require disclosures about subsidiaries, but do not govern the accounting treatment for hedge accounting.
These paragraphs require disclosures about related party transactions, but are not relevant to the hedge accounting treatment.
This Interpretation addresses the measurement of dividend payables, but not the foreign exchange risk on dividend receivables.
These paragraphs require disclosures about financing activities, but do not affect the hedge accounting treatment.

treatment_families.family: Hedge accounting for foreign exchange risk on intragroup monetary items in consolidated financial statements

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 permits the designation of the foreign exchange risk of an intragroup dividend receivable as a hedged item in a fair value hedge, subject to meeting the hedge accounting criteria.

approaches.label: Recognition in profit or loss without hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: If hedge accounting is not applied, exchange differences on the dividend receivable are recognized in profit or loss in accordance with IAS 21 paragraph 28.
